title: fraxinus griffithii
intro: Fraxinus griffithii, also known as Griffith's Ash, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and parts of China. The bark of this plant contains a compound called tremetol, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The bark is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Fraxinus griffithii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its bark can be dried and powdered for use in traditional medicine.
